text.title: BABOK Knowledge Areas — Task Detail
text.summary: Per-KA task tables, key outputs, linked methodologies, and key techniques for all six knowledge areas.

section.title: KA-1: Business Analysis Planning and Monitoring
section.p: Purpose: define approach, identify stakeholders, plan governance.

list.item: Plan BA Approach — how BA will be performed → BA approach document.
Plan Stakeholder Engagement — identify and plan work with stakeholders → stakeholder engagement approach.
Plan Governance — decision-making for requirements → governance approach.
Plan Information Management — how BA info is stored and accessed → information management approach.
Identify Improvements — assess BA performance → BA performance improvements.

section.p: Methodologies: ba-planning, stakeholder-analysis. Outputs: BA approach document, stakeholder register, governance framework, communication plan.

section.title: KA-2: Elicitation and Collaboration
section.p: Purpose: obtain information from stakeholders and confirm results.

list.item: Prepare for Elicitation — scope, technique selection → elicitation activity plan.
Conduct Elicitation — draw out relevant information → elicitation results (unconfirmed).
Confirm Results — verify understanding → elicitation results (confirmed).
Communicate BA Information — shared understanding → BA communication.
Manage Collaboration — encourage stakeholder cooperation → updated stakeholder engagement.

section.p: Methodologies: elicitation-techniques. Techniques: interviews, workshops, document analysis, observation, surveys, prototyping, brainstorming, focus groups.

section.title: KA-3: Requirements Lifecycle Management
section.p: Purpose: manage and maintain requirements throughout the solution lifecycle.

list.item: Trace Requirements — alignment at different levels → requirements (traced).
Maintain Requirements — accurate and consistent → requirements (maintained).
Prioritize Requirements — rank by importance → requirements (prioritized).
Assess Changes — evaluate proposed changes → change assessment.
Approve Requirements — agreement and approval → requirements (approved).

section.p: Methodologies: requirements-traceability, requirements-lifecycle, requirements-prioritization. Outputs: traceability matrix, requirements baseline, prioritized backlog, change impact analysis.

section.title: KA-4: Strategy Analysis
section.p: Purpose: define the future state needed to address business needs.

list.item: Analyze Current State — understand existing environment → current state description.
Define Future State — define goals and objectives → future state description.
Assess Risks — identify uncertainties → risk assessment results.
Define Change Strategy — approach to achieve future state → change strategy.

section.p: Methodologies: strategy-analysis. Techniques: gap analysis, SWOT, business model canvas, capability assessment, risk analysis.

section.title: KA-5: Requirements Analysis and Design Definition
section.p: Purpose: structure, specify, model, verify, validate requirements, and define design options.

list.item: Specify and Model Requirements — → requirements (specified/modeled).
Verify Requirements — quality standards met → requirements (verified).
Validate Requirements — alignment with business goals → requirements (validated).
Define Requirements Architecture — requirements support each other → requirements architecture.
Define Design Options — solution approaches → design options.
Analyze Value and Recommend — assess options → solution recommendation.

section.p: Methodologies: use-case-modeling, user-story-mapping, data-analysis, business-process-analysis, decision-analysis, interface-analysis, requirements-validation, acceptance-criteria, requirements-documentation. Techniques: use cases, user stories, process modeling (BPMN), data modeling (ERD), decision tables, interface specifications, prototyping.

section.title: KA-6: Solution Evaluation
section.p: Purpose: assess solution performance and value delivered.

list.item: Measure Performance — define measurement → solution performance measures.
Analyze Measures — examine data → performance analysis.
Assess Solution Limitations — investigate problems → solution limitations.
Assess Enterprise Limitations — enterprise factor impacts → enterprise limitations.
Recommend Actions — identify value improvements → value improvement recommendations.

section.p: Methodologies: solution-assessment. Outputs: performance metrics, value realization report, improvement recommendations, lessons learned.
